title: Objects, Packfiles, Reachability, and Integrity · History Defense
summary: A maintainer review compares near-miss operations for objects, packfiles, reachability, and integrity and requires the smallest safe object-database integrity and reachability report with topology and content evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for object-database integrity and reachability report. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace blobs, trees, commits, annotated tags, parent edges, and reference roots as an immutable reachable object graph.
Explain loose objects, pack indexes, delta bases, multi-pack indexes, and why storage representation does not change object identity.
Distinguish reachable, unreachable, dangling, promised, missing, and corrupt objects using fsck and reference evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Case G44 is a quiz in a disposable repository. the reported tree is unreachable but still present rather than corrupt or promised. The learner must predict fsck classifies connectivity and names candidate unreachable objects without pruning them, run git fsck --full --no-reflogs --unreachable, explain the candidate tree is present and reachable from the retained incident commit only, test this changed condition: remove the retained incident root in the simulator and observe the commit become unreachable, and preserve this recovery boundary: create refs/recovery/integrity before any repack expiration or prune operation. Competency probe: Quarantine suspect storage, preserve alternate copies and refs, verify object connectivity, and defer destructive pruning until recovery proof exists.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for quarantine suspect storage, preserve alternate copies and refs, verify object connectivity, and defer destructive pruning until recovery proof exists.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Gita Integrity Preservation: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Case G51 is a quiz in a disposable repository. the reported tree is unreachable but still present rather than corrupt or promised. The learner must predict fsck classifies connectivity and names candidate unreachable objects without pruning them, run git fsck --full --no-reflogs --unreachable, explain the candidate tree is present and reachable from the retained incident commit only, test this changed condition: remove the retained incident root in the simulator and observe the commit become unreachable, and preserve this recovery boundary: create refs/recovery/integrity before any repack expiration or prune operation. Competency probe: Quarantine suspect storage, preserve alternate copies and refs, verify object connectivity, and defer destructive pruning until recovery proof exists.
steps.why: Retrieving Gita Integrity Preservation through the concrete object-database integrity and reachability report case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Quarantine suspect storage, preserve alternate copies and refs, verify object connectivity, and defer destructive pruning until recovery proof exists.
